mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) over the prior three months signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year window or positive return frequency below 52% during rising PAYEMS periods.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0 percent signals restrictive monetary policy that raises the discount rate and slows credit creation, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising retail sales (RSAFS) and falling unemployment (UNRATE) signal demand-driven expansion without labor market slack, amplifying corporate earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and UNRATE shows a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) is contracting in a late-cycle expansion, monetary easing via a declining federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a policy pivot that prevents earnings collapse, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with contracting industrial output
failure_condition: Positive 60-day return frequency is below 55% during periods of falling INDPRO and declining FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals durable demand for construction and related credit expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as cyclical sectors reprice.
failure_condition: Rank IC for HOUST over 5-day horizon falls below 0.02 in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year increase in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) exceeding 3.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative return hit rate is below 55% when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) confirms a goods-demand loop where factory output translates directly into revenue, amplifying positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and RSAFS fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over two months, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces household income expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The trigger produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered episodes over a 3-year rolling window.
